hook.text: On October 1, 2013, the U.S. launched HealthCare.gov. It crashed within two hours.

buildup.text: 55 contractors built different pieces with no single integrator. Testing started just two weeks before launch.

discovery.text: On day one, only six people successfully enrolled. Six — out of millions trying.

twist.text: A rescue team of Silicon Valley engineers rebuilt the system in six weeks — working around the clock.

climax.text: The site eventually enrolled millions. But the launch disaster became a textbook project management failure.

punchline.text: Fifty-five contractors, no owner. That was the bug.

quiz.question: How many people enrolled on HealthCare.gov's first day?
